news.title: Trump says rescued U.S. airman 'seriously wounded,' threatens to strike Iran power plants and bridges
news.summary: Trump says the officer was rescued "deep in Enemy Territory" in mountainous terrain. Trump says he was rescued in "enemy territory"

news.title: Chinese chip firms hit record high revenue driven by the AI boom and U.S. curbs
news.summary: Chinese chip companies have benefited from strong domestic demand for AI. U.S. tech curbs have bolstered local firms, according to analysts.

news.title: 'Anything but autos': Can defense save Europe’s ailing car industry?
news.summary: Many of the underlying skills are highly transferable, according to experts. The transition from wheels to weapons is achievable in part because of these skills, experts say.

news.title: 'Silent killers': How AI start-ups are trying to solve one of the retail industry's biggest problems
news.summary: The rapid evolution of generative AI has finally made these applications good enough to meaningfully impact retailers' bottom lines. It is now possible to use AI to predict what customers want to buy and when.

news.title: Warsh nomination moves ahead, putting Trump's competing Fed plans on a collision course
news.summary: A Senate committee will consider Kevin Warsh's nomination even as one of its members still plans to block it. Warsh is a former head of the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. He was nominated by President Barack Obama.

news.title: ‘Our mood changes almost on a daily basis’: Why $4 gas prices feel a lot worse this time around
news.summary: "There is no hive mind. As far as gas prices go, there are no hiveminds," he says. "There is just no reason to be concerned about it"

news.title: ‘This is an overlooked catastrophe’: Why do so many hospitals not accept Medicare Advantage for cancer patients?
news.summary: Insurers have pushed certain cancer-care centers out of network before the end of the calendar or policy year, the company says. “Insurers are pushing certain cancer care centers.” the company adds.

news.title: ‘We’re aiming for a monthly income of $11,500’: I’m 64. I’ve $1.5 million in a 401(k). How do I time my withdrawals?
news.summary: “I plan to start collecting my Social Security of $4,100 at 68,” he says. “I’m going to be able to retire at 68.”

news.title: ‘I feel overwhelmed’: I’m 56 and only have $60,000 in my IRA. Is it too late for me?
news.summary: “My husband has a pension, but I worry that if he passes before me, I could be left with nothing,” she says.

news.title: The 5 highest-paid college basketball players this year: No. 1 is making $4.2 million from NIL
news.summary: Several athletes are now earning over $1 million from NIL deals. As the Final Four of March Madness tips off, multiple athletes are getting paid.
